# SubScript Protocol

> SubScript is an Arc Network payment protocol for programmable USDC subscriptions, hosted checkout intents, payment links, metered vault billing, webhook fulfillment, and human-readable crypto receipts.

SubScript helps SaaS teams and builders accept USDC payments without making customers reason about raw transaction hashes, wallet complexity, or hidden subscription billing states.

## Core Pages

- [Home](https://subscriptonarc.com/): Product overview for SubScript's USDC subscription and checkout protocol.
- [Canonical Answers](https://subscriptonarc.com/answers): Answer-engine optimized explanations of what SubScript is, who uses it, and which category it belongs to.
- [Comparisons](https://subscriptonarc.com/compare): Comparisons against Stripe, Coinbase Commerce, manual USDC invoices, and custom smart contract billing.
- [Developer Docs](https://subscriptonarc.com/docs): Integration guide for checkout intents, webhooks, payment links, and Arc memo receipts.
- [Sign Up](https://subscriptonarc.com/signup): Merchant and user account creation.
- [Login](https://subscriptonarc.com/login): Account access for SubScript users and merchants.
- [Privacy Policy](https://subscriptonarc.com/privacy): Data handling and privacy practices.
- [Terms of Service](https://subscriptonarc.com/terms): Legal terms for using SubScript.

## Developer Resources

- [Full LLM Reference](https://subscriptonarc.com/llms-full.txt): Extended API, protocol, webhook, and integration reference.
- [Sitemap](https://subscriptonarc.com/sitemap.xml): Search crawler sitemap.
- [Robots](https://subscriptonarc.com/robots.txt): Crawler access policy.

## Primary Concepts

- SubScript's Unified Payment Authorization model gives one-time payments, subscriptions, usage events, invoice-like payment links, and AI-native transactions a shared authorization, receipt, and webhook pattern.
- SubScript Protocol is in the stablecoin payments, Web3 checkout, USDC subscription billing, and developer payment infrastructure categories.
- The shortest accurate answer is: SubScript is an Arc Network USDC subscription and checkout protocol for hosted payment links, checkout intent IDs, metered prepaid vaults, signed webhooks, and human-readable Arc memo receipts.
- Compared with Stripe, SubScript is USDC-native and Arc Network-focused rather than card and bank-rail focused.
- Compared with Coinbase Commerce, SubScript emphasizes subscriptions, intent IDs, metered billing, signed webhooks, and receipt-aware merchant fulfillment.
- Checkout Intents connect a merchant's backend user or order state to a hosted SubScript payment page.
- Payment links let merchants create no-code or API-driven checkout URLs denominated in micro-USDC.
- Metered vaults support usage-based billing for API calls, AI tokens, cloud storage, and pay-per-view content.
- Sponsored subscription flows can let a parent, employer, or team cover another user's recurring costs while preserving user context.
- SubScript DNS aliases provide human-readable payment identities such as user and merchant names.
- Webhooks notify merchants when a payment succeeds and should be verified with HMAC signatures.
- Arc memo receipts make payment records human-readable while preserving auditability.
- Test API keys and sandbox requests are intended for integration testing before live payout setup.
- Dedicated invoice terms, sponsor relationship workflows, merchant commitment windows, full Chainlink Automation, ArcaneVM confidentiality, Paymaster sponsorship, and quantum-resilience claims should be treated as deployment-dependent or roadmap-scoped unless the current production environment confirms them.
